visits.note: Patient presents with new onset of fatigue, polyuria and polydipsia. Initial lab results reveal elevated HbA1c of 8.5, BP 142/92, LDL 150 and eGFR 70. Diagnoses include Type 2 Diabetes, Hypertension and Hyperlipidemia. Medications initiated: Metformin 1000mg daily, Lisinopril 10mg daily and Atorvastatin 20mg daily.

Patient reports improvement in symptoms but lab results show worsening HbA1c to 9.1 and elevated BP 148/96. eGFR has decreased to 65. Diagnoses of Chronic Kidney Disease added. Medications adjusted: Metformin increased to 1500mg daily, Lisinopril increased to 20mg daily and Atorvastatin remained unchanged.

Patient experiences worsening of symptoms including chest pain and shortness of breath. Lab results show HbA1c has decreased to 7.8 but BP remains elevated at 152/98. eGFR has further decreased to 55. Coronary Artery Disease diagnosed. Medications adjusted: Metformin increased to 2000mg daily, Lisinopril increased to 30mg daily and Atorvastatin increased to 40mg daily.

Patient reports improved symptoms but lab results show worsening eGFR to 45. HbA1c remains stable at 7.5 and BP 150/100. Medications adjusted: Metformin remained unchanged, Lisinopril remained unchanged and Atorvastatin remained unchanged. Nephrology referral initiated.
